zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow於1310年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福時，他的父親Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow36歲，母親Mary Gordon20歲。他於1335年與Margaret Leslie在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡焦福結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1333年登記服兵役。他去世於1374年在他的家鄉享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow于1310年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾卡焦福时，他的父亲Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow36岁，母亲Mary Gordon20岁。他于1335年与Margaret Leslie在苏格兰藍納可爾卡焦福结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1333年登记服兵役。他去世于1374年在他的家乡享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hamiliton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Fritzwalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow was born in 1310, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow, was 36 and his mother, Mary Gordon, was 20. He married Margaret Leslie in 1335, in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1333. He died in 1374, in his hometown, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow est né en 1310, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Mary Gordon, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Leslie en 1335, à Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1333. Il est décédé en 1374, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow 1310 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow 36 und seine Mutter Mary Gordon 20. Er hat 1335 in Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland Margaret Leslie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1333 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1374 im Alter von 64 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow è nato il nel 1310 nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Mary Gordon, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Leslie nel 1335, nel Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1333. Morì nel 1374, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzowが1310年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカドゾウで生まれた時，父Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzowは36歳，母Mary Gordonは20歳であった。1335年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアカドゾウでMargaret Leslieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1333年に兵役登録をした。彼は1374年に故郷で64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow(이)가 1310년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Gordon(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1335년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 카드즈에서 Margaret Leslie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1374년 그의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow nasceu em 1310, em Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Mary Gordon, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Leslie em 1335, em Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1333. Ele faleceu em 1374, em sua cidade natal, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow родился в 1310 г. в местности кадзоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Gordon, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Leslie в 1335 г. в местности кадзоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. В 1333 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1374 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir David Fritzwalter de Hamiliton 2nd Lord Cadzow nació en 1310, en Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir Walter Fitzgilbert de Hamilton 1st Lord Cadzow, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Mary Gordon, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Margaret Leslie en 1335, en Cadzow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1333. Murió en 1374, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años.
